summary: Minimal developer portfolio website with clean monochromatic design, featuring Hero, About, Skills, Projects, and Contact sections. Mobile responsive with subtle hover effects and optional dark mode.

existing_features.title: Create a Hero section with a name placeholder (e.g., 'Your Name'), a short tagline ('Developer'), and a brief one-liner intro. Layout should be centered with generous white space, no background imagery or gradients.
Create an About section with a short placeholder bio paragraph describing the developer's background and interests.

Create a Skills section displaying a clean list or minimal grid of placeholder tech skills: HTML, CSS, JavaScript, React, Node.js, and at least two others. Each skill should be a simple tag or pill with no heavy decoration.

Create a Projects section with 2–3 placeholder project cards. Each card must include a project title, a short description, a GitHub link, and a Live link. Cards should have a subtle border or shadow and a hover effect (e.g., slight border color change or lift).
Create a Contact section with a placeholder email address and links to GitHub and LinkedIn. Layout should be simple — no form required, just clean inline or stacked links.

Implement a sticky top navigation bar with smooth-scroll anchor links to each section (Hero, About, Skills, Projects, Contact) and a dark mode toggle button. Navigation should collapse gracefully on mobile (hamburger or minimal approach).

Apply a clean, minimal visual design system across the entire portfolio: default light theme with near-black text (#111 or similar), white or off-white backgrounds, one subtle accent color (e.g., a muted teal or slate), clean sans-serif typography (Inter or system-ui font stack), generous white space between sections, and subtle hover effects only. Dark mode should invert the palette cleanly. No heavy animations, gradients, or decorative flourishes.
